mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the risk-free discount rate applied to equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate equity risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive in greater than 55% of instances where UNRATE increases exceed 0.3pp.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer demand, which forces equity market participants to downgrade near-term earnings forecasts and sell S&P 500 positions over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce negative 20-day forward returns with a rank IC below -0.02 over any 24-month period.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly growth in the index of industrial production above 1.0% signals robust manufacturing capacity utilization that supports corporate revenue expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth above 1.0% coincides with negative 60-day forward returns in greater than 60% of observed quarters during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL increase exceeding 0.5% signals accelerating price pressures that force central banks to tighten policy expectations, immediately depressing equity valuations over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in greater than 50% of months where CPIAUCSL rises above 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and a simultaneously rising unemployment rate indicates labor force participation expansion outpacing job creation, which suppresses wage growth expectations and yields negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal produces a positive rank IC greater than 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession expansion regime, quarterly real GDP growth above 2.5% confirms sustainable economic momentum that reduces recession risk premiums and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above 2.5% fails to predict positive 20-day returns with a win rate below 45% during post-recession quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly declines in the index of industrial production signal immediate supply-side bottlenecks and weakening manufacturing output, which trigger algorithmic short-selling and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no significant negative correlation with INDPRO declines over a 24-month rolling evaluation period.
